description.content: Manage comments on issues.
Returns all comments for an issue. Comments are ordered by created date in ascending order by default.

query.description: The index of the first item to return in a page of results (page offset).
The maximum number of items to return per page.
The order of comments returned. Accepted values: created (ascending by default), -created (descending).
Use expand to include additional information about comments in the response. Accepted values: renderedBody.

Search for issues using JQL (Jira Query Language).

item.name: Jira Search for Issues Using Jql (get)

request.name: Jira Search for Issues Using Jql (get)

description.content: Searches for issues using JQL. If the JQL query expression is too large to be encoded as a query parameter, use the POST version of this resource. Sorting is supported via the ORDER BY clause in JQL.

query.description: The JQL query string. For example: project = HSP AND issuetype = Bug. See JQL documentation for syntax.
The index of the first item to return in a page of results (page offset).
The maximum number of items to return per page. The maximum allowed value is determined by the Jira instance configuration, typically 100.
Whether to validate the JQL query and how to handle validation results. Accepted values: strict, warn, none.
A list of fields to return for each issue. Use *all for all fields, *navigable for navigable fields only.
A list of fields to return for each issue. Use *all for all fields, *navigable for navigable fields only.
Use expand to include additional information about issues in the response. Accepted values: renderedFields, names, schema, transitions, operations, editmeta, changelog, versionedRepresentations.
A list of issue properties to return for each issue.
A list of issue properties to return for each issue.
Whether fields in fields are referenced by keys rather than IDs.

variable.description: Your Jira Cloud site subdomain (e.g., your-company).

response.name: Search results returned successfully.

Create, read, update, delete, and transition Jira issues.

Creates an issue or a sub-task from a JSON representation. The fields that can be set on an issue depend on the connected Jira instance configuration, the project, and the issue type. You can determine appropriate field values using the Create Issue Metadata endpoint.

Use expand to include additional information in the response. Accepted values: renderedFields, names, schema, transitions, operations, editmeta, changelog, versionedRepresentations, fields.
A list of issue properties to return for the issue. By default no properties are returned.

Filter results using a literal string match on project name or key.
The type key of the project. Accepted values: business, service_desk, software.
The ID of the project category to filter by.
Filter results by what the user has permission to do. Accepted values: view, browse, edit, create.

The Jira Cloud platform REST API v3 for building apps and integrations with Atlassian Jira. This specification covers core resources including issues, projects, and search (JQL). All responses use Atlassian Document Format (ADF) for rich text fields. Authentication is via OAuth 2.0 (3LO), API tokens with basic auth, or Atlassian Connect JWT.

Contact Support:
 Name: Atlassian Developer Support
 Email: ecosystem@atlassian.com
